generated_note: FOR AI AGENTS: This file is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for cross-video topic correlation. Before ingesting a new video, read this file fully. Normalize each detected phrase (lowercase, trim, collapse internal whitespace/hyphens to single spaces) and look it up in alias_index. If found -> REUSE that slug. If a confident token-overlap match against an existing display_name/alias exists -> REUSE and add the new alias. Otherwise CREATE a new topic. Append a mention (dedupe on video_id+analysis); never rewrite another video's mention. Then bump last_run/last_updated and append one dated line to _agent_log.md. See AGENTS.md for the full protocol.

topics.short_definition: Architectures in which multiple model instances work concurrently — typically an orchestrator/parent agent delegating subtasks to parallel sub-agents — for parallelism, context isolation, and as a form of test-time compute.

mentions.claim: Multi-agent means multiple Claudes working in parallel — a parent/orchestrator delegating to sub-agents (as in Deep Research and Claude Code); it is implemented on top of tool calling and framed as a form of test-time compute, with overbuilt systems failing via communication overhead.

topics.short_definition: The mechanism by which a model takes actions: it emits a structured call against a declared tool, the application executes it, and the result re-enters the context. The substrate beneath agents, sub-agents, retrieval, and MCP.

mentions.claim: Sub-agents are exposed to Claude through the tool-calling framework — a sub-agent 'looks like a tool' that accepts a prompt; tool calling is the communication protocol, with the handler simply backed by another Claude.

topics.short_definition: An open standard for connecting AI applications to external tools, data, and prompts through a uniform interface; the standard way to add custom tools to an agent loop.

mentions.claim: Tools/MCP servers should be designed 1:1 with your UI, not your API — because the model is a user, a tool should return everything at once and minimize round-trips (the Slack three-endpoints example).

topics.short_definition: The architectural spectrum from predefined prompt chains (workflows) to a model run in a self-correcting loop (agents), and the hybrid 'workflows of agents' where each workflow step is itself a closed agent loop.

mentions.claim: Agents (a model in a loop) have largely overtaken workflows for quality-critical tasks because Claude self-corrects; workflows remain best for low-latency single-shot; the emerging pattern is 'workflows of agents' where each step is a closed agent loop that iterates until correct.

topics.short_definition: Deliberately managing what occupies a model's finite context window — including offloading token-heavy subtasks to sub-agents that return only small results — to keep the working (short-term) memory focused. The working-memory layer of agent memory.

mentions.claim: Sub-agents protect the main context: a token-heavy subtask (e.g. locating a class implementation) is offloaded to a sub-agent that returns only the small final answer, preserving the orchestrator's context window; splitting 100–200 tools into ~20-tool buckets applies the same logic to the tool namespace.
